commit.message:
Aktivitetspenger inntektsrapportering (#480)

This pull request introduces support for "inntektsrapportering" (income
reporting) for the "Aktivitetspenger" benefit throughout the codebase.
It adds new Kafka topics, extends enums and services, and implements new
API endpoints and data models to handle income reporting submissions,
including integration with the task management system.

The most important changes are:

**Kafka Topics and Configuration**
* Added three new Kafka topics for handling different stages of
"aktivitetspenger-inntektsrapportering" (`mottatt`, `preprosessert`,
`cleanup`) in `aktivitetspenger-inntektsrapportering-topics.yml`, with
appropriate ACLs and retention policies.
* Registered the new topic in `Topics.kt` and updated the topic
selection logic to handle the new
`AKTIVITETSPENGER_INNTEKTSRAPPORTERING` type.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-81c50ef5bed7712d8e2e6dbe22dd4503af3ab806c70c1d0181b21efab9cad3b2R38)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-81c50ef5bed7712d8e2e6dbe22dd4503af3ab806c70c1d0181b21efab9cad3b2R62)

**Domain Model and API**
* Introduced `AktivitetspengerInntektsrapportering` and
`AktivitetspengerInntektsrapporteringInnsending` data classes to
represent and process income reporting submissions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-4e656c3718b45418384544295f09723b186c40f7b987e71b0ff43c8e733b4db2R1-R17)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c64c0a0381387e10349d506f520be966d0071b378b7835f62a17fd725d7680eaR1-R76)
* Added a new POST endpoint `/inntektsrapportering/innsending` in
`AktivitetspengerController` to receive income reporting submissions.

**Service Layer and Integration**
* Implemented the `inntektrapportering` method in
`AktivitetspengerService` to handle submissions: validates, registers,
sends metrics, and marks related tasks as completed using
`UngBrukerdialogApiService`.
* Integrated with the task management system to fetch task periods and
mark tasks as solved after income reporting is received.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-21a79806cca2803fba2a7999a021f690656e4623a2be288e1e651d4a18f714c9R35)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-21a79806cca2803fba2a7999a021f690656e4623a2be288e1e651d4a18f714c9R76-R121)

**Enums and Supporting Structures**
* Extended `Ytelse`, `YtelseType`, and `KafkaStreamName` enums to
support the new income reporting flow for Aktivitetspenger, including
mapping topics to the new type.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-dbe2ba52d0c42fe8e7f07082cd545c62e2b498177e0b7268ee16f2b13378415cR67)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dbe2ba52d0c42fe8e7f07082cd545c62e2b498177e0b7268ee16f2b13378415cR92)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-eac334b6458b0e89981131a5806709b194ae23b3f970d3dce8ab2315787a9513L187-R194)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-5fbf2360f35cacd01444cd957c7ed98d25c44513834cc9555c1d5664d3e281ccR121-R125)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1b714cf039c2fdec162bc757b2b540d606a799a8e4448e4eb33491d05eafa710R29)
* Updated PDF generator logic to handle the new reporting type.

These changes establish the full technical foundation for receiving,
processing, and tracking "inntektsrapportering" for Aktivitetspenger in
the system.

* AI generated removal of the big sur compatibility logic. Needs vetted and tested

* added detailed plan for getting to swift 6 in stages

* Initial plan

* Stage 2: Convert NetworkAuthManager from actor to class

With SWIFT_DEFAULT_ACTOR_ISOLATION = MainActor, all types are MainActor
by default. The actor's synchronization purpose (single-flight token
refresh) is now provided by MainActor serialization.

Changes:
- actor NetworkAuthManager → class NetworkAuthManager
- bearerAuthSupported(): drop async (no longer needed without actor)
- basicAuthString(): drop nonisolated (no longer an actor)
- JamfProAPIClient: drop await from bearerAuthSupported() calls
- NetworkAuthManagerTests: drop await from bearerAuthSupported() calls

Side effect: Resolves Token.isValid cross-isolation warning because
Token and NetworkAuthManager are now both on MainActor.

* Stage 3a: Remove DispatchQueue.main.async wrappers

With MainActor default isolation, manual dispatch to the main thread
is redundant. All three locations are already MainActor-isolated.

Changes:
- Alert.swift: Remove DispatchQueue.main.async wrapper in display()
- OpenViewController.swift: Remove wrapper in tableView selectionIndexes
- SaveViewController.swift: Remove wrapper in savePressed panel callback

* Stage 3b: Convert UploadManager to async throws

Replace Task/completion handler pattern with direct async throws methods.

- UploadManager.verifyConnection: async throws -> VerificationInfo
- UploadManager.upload: async throws (no completion handler)
- UploadInfoView.verifyConnection: now async, uses try await

feat: agentic checkPreviousError — backtrack to predecessor on system errors

New src/utils/check-error.ts: spins up a lightweight agent session to
read a predecessor's report and determine if it contains a system error
(API failure, timeout, rate limit) vs legitimate content.

Orchestrator calls checkPreviousError between phases:
- Before implementor → checks evaluator/report.md → re-runs evaluator
- Before evaluator → checks implementor/report.md → re-runs implementor
- Before terminator → checks evaluator/report.md → re-runs evaluator

Uses fast heuristics first (string matching), only spins up an agent
for ambiguous cases. No prompt changes needed.

commit.message:
feat: build cache optimization and CI Go version sync (#21)

* feat: replace ldflags version injection with debug.ReadBuildInfo()

Replace build-time ldflags (-X main.Version/Commit/BuildTime) with a
centralized pkg/buildinfo package that reads version, commit, and build
time at startup using Go's built-in debug.ReadBuildInfo() for VCS info
and a /etc/unbounded-net/version file for the release tag.

This improves Docker layer caching: the go build step no longer changes
on every build because the version/commit/time are no longer compiled
into the binary. Only the final version-file layer changes per release.

Changes:
- Add pkg/buildinfo/buildinfo.go with init() that reads VCS info and
  version file
- Update all binaries (controller, node, unping, unroute) to use
  buildinfo.Version/Commit/BuildTime instead of local ldflags vars
- Update kubectl plugin with debug.ReadBuildInfo() fallback (keeps
  ldflags for go install compatibility)
- Dockerfile: remove COMMIT/BUILD_TIME build-args from build stage,
  write VERSION to /etc/unbounded-net/version as final layer
- CI: remove COMMIT and BUILD_TIME docker build-args

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: use go-version-file instead of hardcoded Go version in CI

Replace go-version: "1.26" with go-version-file: go.mod in all
setup-go steps so the CI Go version stays in sync with the project
automatically. No need to update workflows when bumping Go in go.mod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump Go version to 1.26.2

Update go.mod from 1.26.0 to 1.26.2. CI will pick this up
automatically via go-version-file: go.mod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: cache controller-gen binary in CI lint job

Pin controller-gen to v0.20.1 and cache the compiled binary across
CI runs. The codegen staleness check no longer recompiles controller-gen
from source on every run, saving ~30s.

Also avoids using @latest which could cause non-deterministic CI
results if a new controller-gen version changes output format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: upgrade k8s.io deps to v0.35.0 and fix deprecations

Upgrade all k8s.io/* dependencies to v0.35.0 (from v0.29.0 for
cli-runtime and kube-aggregator, already at v0.35.0 for api/client-go).

Add controller-gen v0.20.1 and govulncheck as go tool dependencies
in go.mod, replacing ad-hoc go install in CI and Makefile.

Bump Go version to 1.26.2 for govulncheck compliance.

Fix all deprecation warnings from the client-go upgrade:
- Replace NewSimpleClientset with NewClientset across all test files
- Migrate workqueue.RateLimitingInterface to typed generics
  (TypedRateLimitingInterface[string])
- Update NewNamedRateLimitingQueue to typed config variant
- Remove runtime type assertions in processNextWorkItem (now
  compile-time safe with typed queues)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Microsoft Go builder image, CI improvements, build_only mode

Switch Docker builder stage to the Microsoft Go container image
(mcr.microsoft.com/oss/go/microsoft/golang) for timely patch releases
and FIPS-compliant crypto. The Go major.minor version is parsed from
go.mod via awk and passed as the GO_VERSION build-arg.

CI improvements:
- All setup-go steps use go-download-base-url for Microsoft Go
- Use go tool controller-gen/govulncheck (from go.mod tool deps)
  instead of go install, eliminating separate install/cache steps
- Add build_only workflow_dispatch input that runs the full pipeline
  without pushing images, creating tags, or publishing releases
- Makefile generate target uses go tool controller-gen

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: CI build failures from Microsoft Go systemcrypto

Two fixes for the Microsoft Go migration:

1. Plugin builds: Add GOEXPERIMENT=nosystemcrypto for cross-compilation.
   Microsoft Go defaults to GOEXPERIMENT=systemcrypto which requires
   CGO_ENABLED=1 (OpenSSL), but the plugin uses CGO_ENABLED=0 for
   cross-compiling to darwin/windows. nosystemcrypto falls back to
   pure-Go crypto.

2. Dockerfile: Move version file write before USER controller. The
   mkdir was failing with 'Permission denied' because it ran as the
   non-root controller user.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: share frontend build via artifact, deduplicate CI jobs

Add a dedicated 'Build Frontend' job that builds the frontend once
and uploads pkg/html/dist/ as an artifact. Lint, test, and Docker
build jobs download it instead of each running npm ci + npm run build
independently.

This eliminates Node.js setup and npm ci from 4 jobs (lint, test,
build-amd64, build-arm64), saving ~10-15s per job and reducing
redundant work.

The Go build cache is already shared across runs via setup-go's
cache: true. Within a single run, lint and test compile in parallel
and both benefit from warm caches on subsequent runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: make govulncheck depend on frontend job for consistency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update golangci-lint-action to v9 (Node 24)

Fixes Node.js 20 deprecation warnings in CI. v9.0.0+ uses Node 24.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: consolidate Docker layer cache to single scope per arch

Use a single GHA cache scope per architecture (build-amd64, build-arm64)
instead of separate scopes per target (controller-amd64, node-amd64,
etc.). The controller build writes all layers (mode=max) including
the shared builder stage. The node build reads from the same scope
and only builds its runtime-specific layers.

This halves cache storage usage and ensures the builder stage layers
(tdnf install, go build) are cached across runs. Previously, separate
scopes meant the controller had to rebuild the builder from scratch
on every run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update upload/download-artifact actions to v6.0.0 (Node 24)

Update to the v6.0.0 release SHAs which include Node 24 support,
fixing Buffer() deprecation warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: use buildkit-cache-dance to persist Docker cache mounts

BuildKit cache mounts (--mount=type=cache) are ephemeral and not
preserved in the GHA layer cache. This means the Go build cache
(/root/.cache/go-build) and module cache (/go/pkg/mod) were empty
on every CI run, causing full recompilation (~67s) every time.

Add reproducible-containers/buildkit-cache-dance to inject/extract
cache mount contents via actions/cache. The Go build cache is keyed
on go.sum hash per architecture, so unchanged dependencies hit the
cache and compilation is skipped.

Expected improvement: Docker go build step drops from ~67s to ~5s
on cache hits (only recompiles changed packages).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fix Docker cache mount persistence across CI runs

The previous cache key (go.sum hash only) caused a cache hit on the
second run, which prevented actions/cache from saving updated content.
The cache-dance extraction also skipped because skip-extraction was
set on cache hit.

Fix by:
- Including github.sha in the cache key so each commit gets a fresh
  save, with restore-keys falling back to previous caches
- Removing skip-extraction so cache-dance always extracts after build
- Using the combined actions/cache (not restore-only) so the post step
  saves the extracted content

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* debug: add cache state inspection before and after Docker build

Temporary debug steps to diagnose why BuildKit cache mounts aren't
being reused across CI runs. Shows:
- go-build-cache directory contents (restored from actions/cache)
- BuildKit builder disk usage (cache mount state)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* debug: remove cache-dance, add --progress=plain to Docker builds

Remove buildkit-cache-dance (cache mounts aren't being persisted
correctly). Add --progress=plain to Docker builds to see cache
import/export messages and diagnose why GHA layer cache isn't
persisting across runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: switch to build-push-action for proper GHA cache support

Replace raw docker buildx build shell commands with the official
docker/build-push-action@v7. The raw CLI commands weren't passing
ACTIONS_CACHE_URL and ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN to the BuildKit builder
container, so cache-from/cache-to type=gha was silently ignored.

build-push-action handles this automatically, which should finally
enable layer caching across CI runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update all GitHub Actions to latest versions

- download-artifact: v6 -> v8 (3e5f45b)
- upload-artifact: v6 -> v7 (bbbca2d)
- cache: fix comment v6 -> v5 (668228422, SHA unchanged)
- build-push-action: v7 (d08e5c3, latest SHA)

Fixes Node.js 20 deprecation warnings from download-artifact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add cache-to for node image build

The node build was missing its own cache-to, so the runtime-base
layer (tdnf install) was rebuilt every run (~10s). Now the node
build writes to its own scope (node-{arch}) and reads from both
its own and the controller's scope (build-{arch}).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: have both controller and node write to same cache scope

Both images now write cache-to with mode=max to the same build-{arch}
scope. The node build (which runs second) overwrites the cache with
all layers from ALL stages (builder, runtime-base, node) via mode=max.
On the next run, both controller and node find all shared layers
cached, including runtime-base.

Removed the separate builder step idea since --target builder doesn't
capture the runtime-base stage (parallel, not ancestor).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: split validate (fmt/tidy/codegen) into parallel job

Move gofmt, go mod tidy, and codegen checks from the lint job into a
new 'Validate' job that runs in parallel with Lint, Test, and Security
Scan. The validate job doesn't need the frontend artifact since it
only checks formatting and codegen output.

This reduces the critical path -- fmt/tidy/codegen (~17s) no longer
blocks golangci-lint (~60s) and vice versa.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* debug: add GODEBUG=gocachetest=1 to test job

Temporary debug flag to see Go test cache hit/miss rate in CI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: explicit Go build/mod caching with job-specific keys

Replace setup-go's built-in caching with explicit actions/cache steps
that cache ~/.cache/go-build, ~/.cache/ms-go-build (Microsoft Go),
and ~/go/pkg/mod.

Each job gets its own cache key scoped by:
- runner.os
- Go version (hashFiles('go.mod'))
- job name (validate/lint/test/vulncheck/assets/plugin)
- dependency hash (hashFiles('**/go.sum'))

This ensures:
- Go version bumps invalidate the cache automatically
- Each job has its own build cache (different build flags produce
  different artifacts)
- restore-keys allow partial cache hits when dependencies change
- Microsoft Go's separate build cache dir is included

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: run build and plugin jobs in parallel with checks

Move build and plugin jobs to run in parallel with validate/lint/test/
govulncheck instead of waiting for them. Build depends on frontend
(needs embedded dist), plugin depends only on version (no frontend).

Manifest, assets, and release jobs still gate on all checks + builds,
so nothing is published if a check fails.

This saves ~2-3 minutes on the happy path by overlapping the build
(~40s per arch) and plugin (~50s per platform) with the longest
check (test ~130s).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: exclude non-build files from Docker context

Add .github/, docs/, deploy/, dev/, build/, and other non-source files
to .dockerignore. Changes to CI workflows, documentation, or deploy
manifests were invalidating the COPY . . layer, causing Go binaries
to be recompiled even when no Go source changed.

The Docker build only needs: Go source (pkg/, cmd/, bpf/), go.mod,
go.sum, resources/ (CNI plugins), pkg/html/dist/ (frontend), and
scripts/node-agent-init.sh.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move node-agent-init.sh to docker/, exclude scripts/

Move node-agent-init.sh from scripts/ to docker/ so scripts/ can be
added to .dockerignore. Reduces the Docker build context and prevents
unrelated script changes from busting the build cache.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: merge manifest rendering into validate job

Move the Kubernetes manifest render and upload steps into the validate
job, eliminating the standalone assets job. The render takes <1s but
the assets job spent ~17s on runner setup, checkout, and Go install.

The validate job already has checkout + setup-go, so the manifest
render adds negligible overhead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: only inject version ldflags for release plugin builds

Drop Commit and BuildTime ldflags from plugin builds entirely --
debug.ReadBuildInfo() provides both from VCS info. Only inject
Version via ldflags on release builds (is_release == true).

For PR builds, ldflags are just -s -w (strip/no-dwarf), making the
build fully cacheable across commits. The plugin shows 'dev' for
version on PR builds, with commit and build time from VCS metadata.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct Docker Go build cache path for Microsoft Go

Microsoft Go uses /root/.cache/ms-go-build instead of the upstream
/root/.cache/go-build. The cache mount was targeting the wrong path,
so the Go build cache was never populated or hit inside Docker.

This explains why the go build step always took ~67s even with cache
mounts -- the cache was being written to the wrong directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: simplify release job dependencies, rename to Create Release

Release job now needs only [version, manifest] -- manifest already
transitively depends on all checks, builds, and plugins. Renamed
from 'Create Draft Release' since it handles both draft and full
releases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
EXP-1559: Meal detection sensitivity analysis (3 configs)

Compare three meal detection configurations across 11 patients:
  A (Census):  ≥5g carbs, 30-min gap → 4,072 meals, quality 0.923
  B (Medium):  ≥5g carbs, 90-min gap → 3,272 meals, quality 0.973
  C (Therapy): ≥18g carbs, 90-min gap → 2,632 meals, quality 0.979

Key findings:
- 90-min hysteresis removes 20% of meals, boosts quality significantly
- ≥18g threshold removes 35% total but achieves 99.2% excellent grade
- Patient impact varies: a loses 72%, e loses only 7%
- Excursion-carb relationship stable across configs (~85 mg/dL for ≥50g)

Parameterized detect_meal_windows() with min_carbs and cluster_gap kwargs.
Added fig8_meal_sensitivity.png visualization (4-panel comparison).

Skip Start_Disposed_ThrowsObjectDisposedException on Apple mobile platforms (#126655)

## Fix

Adds `[SkipOnPlatform(TestPlatforms.iOS | TestPlatforms.tvOS |
TestPlatforms.MacCatalyst)]` to
`Start_Disposed_ThrowsObjectDisposedException`.

## Why

`Process.Start()` on iOS/tvOS/MacCatalyst throws
`PlatformNotSupportedException` (process launching is unsupported)
**before** checking disposal state. The test expects
`ObjectDisposedException` which never fires, causing a deterministic
failure across **7 legs** in every `runtime-extra-platforms` rolling
build.

This follows the same `[SkipOnPlatform]` pattern used by 20+ other tests
in the same file.

Fixes #126654

commit.message:
fix(update): immediate GitHub poll + correct manifest version

Two root causes for the integration update showing 'Unknown':

1. Missing update_before_add=True — the entity started with
   latest_version=None and would not poll GitHub for up to 1 hour
   (SCAN_INTERVAL). Every HA restart reset the timer, so on systems
   that restart regularly the entity never left 'Unknown'.
   Fix: async_add_entities([entity], update_before_add=True) so the
   entity polls GitHub immediately on setup.

2. manifest.json version was frozen at 0.3.0 — every release zip
   contained the wrong installed_version. release.sh already calls
   set_version.sh which correctly bumps the version; this commit
   updates manifest.json to 0.6.0 (the current latest tag) so the
   file is now in sync with the released state.

The release.sh + set_version.sh pipeline already handles future
releases correctly — no additional changes needed there.

Tests: updated async_setup_entry smoke test to accept **kwargs from
update_before_add; added new test asserting update_before_add=True
is always passed. 181/181 passing.

commit.message:
security: rate-limit gossip spam — disconnect on collateral hijack, track AV3 cycling violations

- Add RegistryError::IpCyclingRejected distinct from InvalidCollateral
- Return IpCyclingRejected from AV3 IP-cycling lockout path so violations
  can be tracked separately from other InvalidCollateral rejections
- Propagate disconnect signal from CollateralAlreadyLocked handlers in
  both paid-tier and free-tier announcement paths: record_severe_violation
  return value was previously discarded, leaving the connection alive after
  the 1-hour ban was placed
- Add IpCyclingRejected match arms in both handlers: calls record_violation
  (3-strike escalating ban) and disconnects when should_ban returns true

Before: 50.28.106.227 and 133.18.180.117 could spam 15+ gossip rejections
per 30s window indefinitely — violation was recorded but connection stayed
alive and blacklist check on messages was not applied to active connections.
After: first CollateralAlreadyLocked → 1h ban + disconnect; third AV3
cycling rejection → 1min ban + disconnect.

commit.message:
feat(athena): emit VERIFICATION_PROSE_REWRITTEN mutation events for verification_commands prose rewrites (#279)

Extend buildPatchedPlanCorrectionTracking to detect when verification_commands
or verificationCommands array fields change from prose (non-executable) entries
to executable commands, and emit structured VERIFICATION_PROSE_REWRITTEN
mutation events with field, before, and after payloads.

Previously only the generic 'repaired' legacy correction string was emitted via
the TRACKED_FIELDS loop for these array fields, giving postmortems a false
clean-pass signal when all entries were prose-to-executable rewrites.

The verification (string) field already had this detection. This change mirrors
that behavior for the array fields so correction tracking is symmetric.

Add two deterministic tests:
- Positive: prose verification_commands array → executable emits mutation event
- Negative: already-executable commands changing do not emit VERIFICATION_PROSE_REWRITTEN

[rocshmem] functional_tests/driver.sh: add feature to specify custom test config (#4700)

## Motivation

It is not currently possible to use the functional test driver script
to run a single test with a custom configuration.
This PR adds this feature, which can be used as follows:
```Shell
# rocshmem_functional_driver.sh <test_executable> "<test_name> <ranks> <workgroups> <threads> [max_msg_size]" <log_dir>
cd rocshmem_build_dir
mkdir functional_test_logs
# putnbi test with 2 PEs, 32 workgroups, 256 threads per workgroup, and up to 512B per message
./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_driver.sh ./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_tests "putnbi 2 32 256 512" functional_test_logs
less functional_test_logs/putnbi_n2_w32_z256_512B.log
# amo_finc test with 2 PEs, 1 workgroup, and 1024 threads per workgroup
./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_driver.sh ./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_tests "amo_finc 2 1 1024" functional_test_logs
less functional_test_logs/amo_finc_n2_w1_z1024.log
```

## Technical Details

Extend the driver script to handle arbitrary single test configurations
in the same format as used for input to the ExecTest script function:
* If `$TEST` doesn't match any preset,
  `$TEST` is parsed into a bash array `TEST_OPTS=($TEST)`;
  this is implicitly split at whitespace boundary by bash
* Index 0 is the test name, `NAME=${TEST_OPTS[0]}`
* If the length of the array is 4 or 5 then indices 1–4 are parsed
  as ranks, workgroups, threads, and max message size
* Otherwise the same static default configuration as before is used:
  2 ranks, 1 workgroup, 1 thread, and a max message size of 8B

## JIRA ID

AIROCSHMEM-356

## Test Plan

Run a variety of test configurations using the new custom test feature.

## Test Result

Feature has been in internal use for a few months with no apparent
issues or regressions.

## Submission Checklist

- [x] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

fix(task-graph): resolve race condition in GraphAsTask.executeStream() (#378)

* fix(task-graph): resolve race condition in GraphAsTask.executeStream()

Replace lazy resolveWaiting pattern with eager promise/resolver ("notify")
to prevent generator hangs when producers signal before the resolver is
assigned or after it becomes stale.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JcBKwbGaP193SCNWQhZF8z

* fix(task-graph): address review comments on GraphAsTask.executeStream()

- Fix rejection propagation: runPromise now uses .then(onFulfill, onReject)
  so that a subgraph failure also sets subgraphDone=true and calls notify(),
  preventing the generator from hanging indefinitely on failure.
- Optimize notify(): add isWaiting/hasPending flags so Promise.withResolvers()
  is only called when the generator is actually suspended, avoiding unnecessary
  allocations during high-frequency streaming.
- Add regression tests: RapidStreamTask (8 no-sleep chunks) verifies all events
  are forwarded; AlwaysFailTask verifies rejection unblocks the generator.

commit.message:
fix: write clean report on error, not error details

On system error (API failure, timeout), write 'Initial State' to
evaluator/report.md instead of the error message. Prevents the
implementor from wasting an iteration interpreting 'AI model
connection failure' as evaluator feedback.

Error details are recorded in state.json history — they don't
belong in the evaluator report that agents read.
